actors.text: True population mean mu=10. Sample-mean estimates from many samples are shown. Is the estimator unbiased?
Unbiased: average of estimates centers at mu=10
Biased: estimates are not all exactly 10
actors.reflection: Unbiased does NOT mean every estimate equals mu. It means the AVERAGE of the estimates equals mu. Variability around mu is expected and is captured by the SD of the sampling distribution.

actors.text: Biased: a few estimates landed below 10
actors.reflection: An unbiased estimator can land below or above mu on any single sample. What matters is the LONG-RUN AVERAGE of the estimates, which here is essentially 10.

completion.rule: any correct QuestionDoor switch pressed by >= 1/3 of online players, then any Player walks onto Goal
